CONTENT.Club career: Kangwa began his career at Lusaka-based club Happy Hearts. In 2017, Kangwa signed for Israeli club Hapoel Be'er Sheva, before returning to Happy Hearts in 2018. Ahead of the 2019 Zambian Super League season, Kangwa signed for Buildcon. On 10 July 2019, Kangwa joined Russian club Arsenal Tula. On 29 May 2022, Kangwa joined Serbian club Red Star Belgrade.On 1 February 2024, Kangwa joined Kortrijk in Belgium on loan.
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 30 May 2022As of match played 17 January 2024Scores and results list Zambia's goal tally first, score column indicates score after each Kangwa goal.
CONTENT.International career: In November 2018, Kangwa was called up by Zambia U20 ahead of the 2018 COSAFA U-20 Cup.On 9 June 2019, Kangwa made his debut for Zambia in a 2–1 loss against Cameroon. On 16 July 2019, Kangwa scored his first goal for Zambia in a 3–2 win against Morocco.

SUMMARY: Kings Kangwa (born 6 April 1999) is a Zambian professional footballer who plays as a central midfielder for Israeli club  Hapoel Be'er Sheva and the Zambia national team.
